Optional: {} networkIPv6 string NetworkIPv6 is the IPv6 CIDR of the referenced Network (spec.ipv6.cidr).Empty when the Network has no IPv6 pool or cannot be resolved. Optional: {} vrfs string array VRFs lists the VRF names this attachment is plumbed into, derived from thematched Destinations (spec.destinations → Destination.spec.vrfRef). Sortedand de-duplicated. Optional: {} sriovVlanID integer SRIOVVlanID is the VLAN ID assigned for SR-IOV device traffic. Optional: {} anycast AnycastStatus Anycast holds anycast gateway information. Optional: {} nodeAddresses object (keys:string, values:AddressAllocation) NodeAddresses holds per-node IP addresses allocated when nodeIPs.enabled is true.Key is node name, value contains the allocated addresses. Optional: {} conditions Condition array Conditions represent the latest available observations of the resource's state. Optional: {}

docs.title: LoopbackConfig
docs.text:
LoopbackConfig defines a loopback interface whose per-node source address is allocated from a CIDR by the intent controller. Allocations are persisted in Collector.status.nodeAddresses and remain stable across reconciles unless the node is removed from the cluster.

Appears in: - MirrorVRFRef

Field Description Default Validation name string Name is the loopback interface name (e.g. "lo.mir"). MinLength: 1 Required: {} subnet string Subnet is the CIDR from which per-node loopback source addresses areallocated by the intent controller. Each in-scope node receives onehost address from this subnet, recorded in Collector.status.nodeAddresses.The subnet must be large enough to accommodate every node in scope; anIPv4 /29 yields 6 usable hosts after skipping network and broadcast. MinLength: 1 Required: {}

docs.title: MirrorSource
docs.text:
MirrorSource identifies an attachment to mirror traffic from.

Appears in: - TrafficMirrorSpec

Field Description Default Validation kind string Kind is the type of attachment. Enum: [Layer2Attachment Inbound Outbound] Required: {} name string Name is the name of the attachment resource. MinLength: 1 Required: {}

docs.title: MirrorVRFRef
docs.text:
MirrorVRFRef references a VRF CRD for the mirror VRF and specifies its loopback config.

Appears in: - CollectorSpec

Field Description Default Validation name string Name of the VRF resource. MinLength: 1 Required: {} loopback LoopbackConfig Loopback defines a loopback interface backed by a CAPI IPAM pool for per-nodeGRE source IP allocation. The controller provisions IPAddressClaims from thereferenced pool on each node in scope. Required: {}

docs.title: Network
docs.text:
Network is the Schema for the networks API. A Network represents a pure pool definition referenced by name via networkRef from usage CRDs such as Layer2Attachment, Inbound, Outbound, and PodNetwork.

Field Description Default Validation apiVersion string network-connector.sylvaproject.org/v1alpha1 kind string Network metadata ObjectMeta Refer to Kubernetes API documentation for fields of metadata. spec NetworkSpec status NetworkStatus

docs.title: NetworkSpec
docs.text:
NetworkSpec defines the desired state of Network. A Network is a pure pool definition — CIDR, VLAN, VNI, allocation properties. It does not carry VRFs, node scope, or usage semantics.

Appears in: - Network

Field Description Default Validation ipv4 IPNetwork IPv4 is the IPv4 address pool for this network. The CIDR must be thenetwork address (host bits zero), e.g. "198.51.100.224/27" — not anauthored host address like "198.51.100.225/27". The anycast gateway isderived as the first usable host (network address + 1). Optional: {} ipv6 IPNetwork IPv6 is the IPv6 address pool for this network. The CIDR must be thenetwork address (host bits zero), e.g. "2001:db8::/64". Optional: {} vlan integer VLAN is the VLAN ID for this network. Maximum: 4094 Minimum: 1 Optional: {} vni integer VNI is the VXLAN Network Identifier for this network.In BM4X (bare-metal) mode without SR-IOV, the VNI is provided by theservice integration engineer. The node VTEP IP comes from the underlay,not from this CRD. Maximum: 1.6777215e+07 Minimum: 1 Optional: {}

docs.title: NetworkStatus
docs.text:
NetworkStatus defines the observed state of Network.

Appears in: - Network

Field Description Default Validation observedGeneration integer ObservedGeneration is the most recent generation observed by the controller. referenceCount integer ReferenceCount is the number of usage CRDs that reference this Network. conditions Condition array Conditions represent the latest available observations of the Network's state. Optional: {}

docs.title: NextHopConfig
docs.text:
NextHopConfig specifies next-hop addresses for static routing in non-HBN mode. At least one of IPv4 or IPv6 must be set.

Appears in: - DestinationSpec

Field Description Default Validation ipv4 string IPv4 is the IPv4 next-hop address (e.g. "198.51.100.1"). Optional: {} ipv6 string IPv6 is the IPv6 next-hop address (e.g. "2001:db8:100::1"). Optional: {}

docs.title: NodeAttachment
docs.text:
NodeAttachment attaches cluster nodes to a remote VRF. Node IPs (from node.status.addresses) are exported as host routes into the VRF, and the VRF's destination prefixes are imported back for the attached nodes via SBR.

Field Description Default Validation apiVersion string network-connector.sylvaproject.org/v1alpha1 kind string NodeAttachment metadata ObjectMeta Refer to Kubernetes API documentation for fields of metadata. spec NodeAttachmentSpec status NodeAttachmentStatus

docs.title: NodeAttachmentSpec
docs.text:
NodeAttachmentSpec defines the desired state of NodeAttachment. A NodeAttachment leaks node IPs into a remote VRF and imports remote prefixes back into the cluster VRF via source-based routing.

Appears in: - NodeAttachment

Field Description Default Validation vrfRef string VRFRef references a VRF CRD by name. The VRF provides the L3VNI androute target for the fabric VRF that node IPs will be exported into. MinLength: 1 Required: {} destinations LabelSelector Destinations selects Destination CRDs whose prefixes should be importedback into the cluster VRF (via SBR) for the attached nodes. Required: {} nodeSelector LabelSelector NodeSelector restricts which nodes are attached. If omitted, all nodesare attached to the VRF. Optional: {}

docs.title: NodeAttachmentStatus
docs.text:
NodeAttachmentStatus defines the observed state of NodeAttachment.

Appears in: - NodeAttachment

Field Description Default Validation observedGeneration integer ObservedGeneration is the most recent generation observed by the controller. vrfs string array VRFs lists the VRF names whose prefixes are imported by this attachment,derived from the matched Destinations (spec.destinations →Destination.spec.vrfRef). This is distinct from spec.vrfRef (the targetVRF the nodes attach to). Sorted and de-duplicated. Optional: {} conditions Condition array Conditions represent the latest available observations of the NodeAttachment's state. Optional: {}

docs.title: NodeIPConfig
docs.text:
NodeIPConfig defines node IP assignment configuration for a Layer2Attachment.

Appears in: - Layer2AttachmentSpec

Field Description Default Validation enabled boolean Enabled controls whether node IPs are assigned from the referenced Network. reservedRanges string array ReservedRanges lists IP ranges (CIDRs) within the Network that are reservedfor pod use and must not be allocated to nodes. Each entry must be a validCIDR that falls within the parent Network's address space. Optional: {}

docs.title: NodeInterface
docs.text:
NodeInterface describes a network interface on a node.

Appears in: - NodeNetworkStatusStatus

Field Description Default Validation name string Name is the interface name. mac string Mac is the MAC address of the interface. Optional: {} mtu integer Mtu is the maximum transmission unit. Optional: {} state NodeInterfaceState State is the link state. Enum: [up down unknown] type NodeInterfaceType Type is the interface type. Enum: [physical bond vlan bridge vxlan loopback virtual] Optional: {} members string array Members lists bonded member interfaces (for type=bond). Optional: {} parent string Parent is the parent interface (for type=vlan). Optional: {} vlanID integer VlanID is the VLAN ID (for type=vlan). Optional: {} addresses string array Addresses lists assigned IP addresses. Optional: {}

docs.title: NodeInterfaceState
docs.text:
Underlying type: string

NodeInterfaceState describes the link state of an interface.

Validation: - Enum: [up down unknown]

Appears in: - NodeInterface

Field Description up down unknown

docs.title: NodeInterfaceType
docs.text:
Underlying type: string

NodeInterfaceType describes the type of a network interface.

Validation: - Enum: [physical bond vlan bridge vxlan loopback virtual]

Appears in: - NodeInterface

Field Description physical bond vlan bridge vxlan loopback virtual

docs.title: NodeNetworkStatus
docs.text:
NodeNetworkStatus is the Schema for the nodenetworkstatuses API. It represents per-node network inventory populated by CRA agents.

Field Description Default Validation apiVersion string network-connector.sylvaproject.org/v1alpha1 kind string NodeNetworkStatus metadata ObjectMeta Refer to Kubernetes API documentation for fields of metadata. spec NodeNetworkStatusSpec status NodeNetworkStatusStatus

docs.title: NodeNetworkStatusSpec
docs.text:
NodeNetworkStatusSpec is intentionally empty; the resource is agent-populated.

Appears in: - NodeNetworkStatus

docs.title: NodeNetworkStatusStatus
docs.text:
NodeNetworkStatusStatus contains the observed network state of a node.

Appears in: - NodeNetworkStatus

Field Description Default Validation interfaces NodeInterface array Interfaces lists the network interfaces on the node. Optional: {} routes NodeRoute array Routes lists the routing table entries on the node. Optional: {} lastUpdated Time LastUpdated is the timestamp of the last status update. Optional: {} conditions Condition array Conditions represent the latest available observations of the resource's state. Optional: {}

docs.title: NodeRoute
docs.text:
NodeRoute describes a routing table entry on a node.

Appears in: - NodeNetworkStatusStatus

Field Description Default Validation destination string Destination is the route destination in CIDR notation. gateway string Gateway is the next-hop gateway address. Optional: {} interface string Interface is the egress interface for this route. table string Table is the routing table name. Optional: {}

docs.title: Outbound
docs.text:
Outbound enables egress via SNAT, allocating IPs from a Network for Coil Egress and Calico pools.

Field Description Default Validation apiVersion string network-connector.sylvaproject.org/v1alpha1 kind string Outbound metadata ObjectMeta Refer to Kubernetes API documentation for fields of metadata. spec OutboundSpec status OutboundStatus

docs.title: OutboundSpec
docs.text:
OutboundSpec defines the desired state of Outbound. Enables egress via SNAT, allocating IPs from a Network for Coil Egress and Calico pools.

Appears in: - Outbound

Field Description Default Validation networkRef string NetworkRef references a Network CRD by name. MinLength: 1 Required: {} destinations LabelSelector Destinations selects Destination CRDs. Optional: {} count integer Count is the number of IPs to allocate. Mutually exclusive with Addresses. Minimum: 1 Optional: {} addresses AddressAllocation Addresses specifies explicit address allocations. Mutually exclusive with Count. Optional: {} replicas integer Replicas is the number of Coil egress pod replicas. Minimum: 1 Optional: {}

docs.title: OutboundStatus
docs.text:
OutboundStatus defines the observed state of Outbound.

Appears in: - Outbound

Field Description Default Validation observedGeneration integer ObservedGeneration is the most recent generation observed by the controller. addresses AddressAllocation Addresses holds the allocated IP addresses. Optional: {} vrfs string array VRFs lists the VRF names this Outbound is plumbed into, derived from thematched Destinations (spec.destinations → Destination.spec.vrfRef). Sortedand de-duplicated. Optional: {} conditions Condition array Conditions represent the latest available observations of the Outbound's state. Optional: {}

docs.title: PodNetwork
docs.text:
PodNetwork is the Schema for the podnetworks API. It allocates additional pod-level networks from a Network for CNI integration.

Field Description Default Validation apiVersion string network-connector.sylvaproject.org/v1alpha1 kind string PodNetwork metadata ObjectMeta Refer to Kubernetes API documentation for fields of metadata. spec PodNetworkSpec status PodNetworkStatus

docs.title: PodNetworkSpec
docs.text:
PodNetworkSpec defines the desired state of PodNetwork.

Appears in: - PodNetwork

Field Description Default Validation networkRef string NetworkRef is the name of the Network resource this PodNetwork allocates from. MinLength: 1 Required: {} destinations LabelSelector Destinations selects the destination workloads that may use this pod network.

docs.title: PodNetworkStatus
docs.text:
PodNetworkStatus defines the observed state of PodNetwork.

Appears in: - PodNetwork

Field Description Default Validation observedGeneration integer ObservedGeneration is the most recent generation observed by the controller. networkIPv4 string NetworkIPv4 is the IPv4 CIDR of the referenced Network (spec.ipv4.cidr).Empty when the Network has no IPv4 pool or cannot be resolved. Optional: {} networkIPv6 string NetworkIPv6 is the IPv6 CIDR of the referenced Network (spec.ipv6.cidr).Empty when the Network has no IPv6 pool or cannot be resolved. Optional: {} vrfs string array VRFs lists the VRF names this PodNetwork is plumbed into, derived from thematched Destinations (spec.destinations → Destination.spec.vrfRef). Sortedand de-duplicated. Optional: {} conditions Condition array Conditions represent the latest available observations of thePodNetwork's current state. Optional: {}

docs.title: PortRange
docs.text:
PortRange defines an inclusive start–end port range.

Appears in: - DestinationPort

Field Description Default Validation start integer Start is the first port in the range (1–65535). Maximum: 65535 Minimum: 1 end integer End is the last port in the range (≥ Start, 1–65535). Maximum: 65535 Minimum: 1

docs.title: RouteAnnouncementConfig
docs.text:
RouteAnnouncementConfig configures communities for a class of routes.

Appears in: - AnnouncementPolicySpec

Field Description Default Validation communities string array Communities lists BGP community strings to attach to these routes. Optional: {}

docs.title: SRIOVConfig
docs.text:
SRIOVConfig defines SR-IOV configuration for a Layer2Attachment. When enabled, the agent performs SR-IOV VF passthrough instead of creating VXLAN/VLAN interfaces via CRA. The CRA bridge and VXLAN setup is skipped entirely for SR-IOV attachments.

Appears in: - Layer2AttachmentSpec

Field Description Default Validation enabled boolean Enabled controls whether SR-IOV is active. Immutable once set.

docs.title: TrafficMatch
docs.text:
TrafficMatch optionally filters which traffic to mirror.

Appears in: - TrafficMirrorSpec

Field Description Default Validation srcPrefix string SrcPrefix filters by source IP prefix in CIDR notation. Optional: {} dstPrefix string DstPrefix filters by destination IP prefix in CIDR notation. Optional: {} protocol string Protocol filters by IP protocol. Enum: [TCP UDP ICMP] Optional: {} srcPort integer SrcPort filters by source port (requires protocol to be tcp or udp). Maximum: 65535 Minimum: 1 Optional: {} dstPort integer DstPort filters by destination port (requires protocol to be tcp or udp). Maximum: 65535 Minimum: 1 Optional: {}

docs.title: TrafficMirror
docs.text:
TrafficMirror declaratively mirrors traffic from an attachment to a Collector.

Field Description Default Validation apiVersion string network-connector.sylvaproject.org/v1alpha1 kind string TrafficMirror metadata ObjectMeta Refer to Kubernetes API documentation for fields of metadata. spec TrafficMirrorSpec status TrafficMirrorStatus

docs.title: TrafficMirrorSpec
docs.text:
TrafficMirrorSpec defines the desired state of TrafficMirror.

Appears in: - TrafficMirror

Field Description Default Validation source MirrorSource Source identifies the attachment to mirror traffic from. Required: {} collector string Collector is the name of the Collector resource. MinLength: 1 Required: {} direction string Direction is the mirror direction. Defaults to "both" to match the underlyingNodeNetworkConfig MirrorACL behavior when the field is omitted. both Enum: [ingress egress both] Optional: {} trafficMatch TrafficMatch TrafficMatch optionally filters which traffic to mirror. Optional: {}

docs.title: TrafficMirrorStatus
docs.text:
TrafficMirrorStatus defines the observed state of TrafficMirror.

Appears in: - TrafficMirror

Field Description Default Validation observedGeneration integer ObservedGeneration is the most recent generation observed by the controller. activeNodes integer ActiveNodes is the number of nodes where the mirror is active. conditions Condition array Conditions represent the latest available observations of the TrafficMirror's state. Optional: {}

docs.title: VRF
docs.text:
VRF represents a backbone VRF identity — name and overlay parameters (VNI, route target). Defined once per VRF, referenced by name from Destination resources.

Field Description Default Validation apiVersion string network-connector.sylvaproject.org/v1alpha1 kind string VRF metadata ObjectMeta Refer to Kubernetes API documentation for fields of metadata. spec VRFSpec status VRFStatus

docs.title: VRFSpec
docs.text:
VRFSpec defines the desired state of VRF.

Appears in: - VRF

Field Description Default Validation vrf string VRF is the name of the VRF in the backbone. It may be a readable name; thecontroller reduces it to a datapath-safe form (<=15 chars). The webhookrejects names that cannot be reduced to fit. The generous upper bound hereis only a sanity limit. MaxLength: 63 Required: {} vni integer VNI is the VXLAN Network Identifier. When omitted, the controller resolves it from operator config. Maximum: 1.6777215e+07 Minimum: 1 Optional: {} routeTarget string RouteTarget is the BGP route target for the VRF. When omitted, the controller resolves it. Optional: {}

docs.title: VRFStatus
docs.text:
VRFStatus defines the observed state of VRF.

Appears in: - VRF

Field Description Default Validation observedGeneration integer ObservedGeneration is the most recent generation observed by the controller. referenceCount integer ReferenceCount is the number of Destinations that reference this VRF. references string array References lists the names of Destinations that reference this VRF. Optional: {} conditions Condition array Conditions represent the latest available observations of the VRF's state. Optional: {}

docs.title: Prometheus Metrics
docs.text: The network operator exposes Prometheus metrics for observability. All metrics use the nwop_ namespace.

docs.title: Health check metrics
docs.text:
Metric Type Labels Description nwop_healthcheck_status Gauge check Health check status (1=passing, 0=failing) nwop_healthcheck_last_success_timestamp_seconds Gauge check Unix timestamp of last successful check nwop_healthcheck_duration_seconds Histogram check Duration of health check execution nwop_healthcheck_node_ready Gauge reason Node readiness condition (1=ready, 0=not ready) nwop_healthcheck_taints_removed Gauge – Whether init taints have been removed (1=yes, 0=no)

The check label can be interfaces, reachability, or apiserver.

docs.title: Scrape metrics
docs.text: Metric Type Labels Description nwop_scrape_collector_duration_seconds Gauge collector Duration of a collector scrape nwop_scrape_collector_success Gauge collector Whether a collector succeeded (1=yes, 0=no)

docs.title: Netlink collector metrics (agent-cra-frr, frr-exporter)
docs.text: Metric Type Labels Description nwop_netlink_routes_fib Gauge table, vrf, protocol, address_family Number of routes in the Linux dataplane nwop_netlink_neighbors Gauge interface, address_family, flags, status Number of neighbors in the Linux dataplane

docs.text: Metric Type Labels Description nwop_frr_routes_fib Gauge table, vrf, protocol, address_family Routes in FRR FIB nwop_frr_routes_rib Gauge table, vrf, protocol, address_family Routes in FRR RIB nwop_frr_vni_state Gauge table, vrf, svi, vtep VRF/EVPN VNI state nwop_frr_bgp_uptime_seconds_total Counter vrf, as, peer_name, peer_host, ip_family, message_type, subsequent_family, remote_as BGP session uptime nwop_frr_bgp_status Gauge (same as above) BGP session status (1=established, 0=down) nwop_frr_bgp_prefixes_received_total Counter (same as above) Prefixes received from peer nwop_frr_bgp_prefixes_transmitted_total Counter (same as above) Prefixes transmitted to peer nwop_frr_bgp_messages_received_total Counter (same as above) Messages received from peer nwop_frr_bgp_messages_transmitted_total Counter (same as above) Messages transmitted to peer

docs.text: Metric Type Labels Description nwop_vsr_routes_fib Gauge table, vrf, protocol, address_family Routes in VSR FIB nwop_vsr_routes_rib Gauge table, vrf, protocol, address_family Routes in VSR RIB nwop_vsr_vni_state Gauge table, vrf, svi, vtep VRF VNI state nwop_vsr_bgp_uptime_seconds_total Counter vrf, as, peer_name, ip_family, message_type, subsequent_family, remote_as BGP session uptime nwop_vsr_bgp_status Gauge (same as above) BGP session status nwop_vsr_bgp_prefixes_received_total Counter (same as above) Prefixes received from peer nwop_vsr_bgp_prefixes_transmitted_total Counter (same as above) Prefixes transmitted to peer nwop_vsr_bgp_messages_received_total Counter (same as above) Messages received from peer nwop_vsr_bgp_messages_transmitted_total Counter (same as above) Messages transmitted to peer nwop_vsr_neighbors Gauge interface, address_family, flags, status Neighbor count

docs.title: Node Readiness
docs.text: During initialization, nodes may be tainted to prevent premature workload scheduling while the network stack is being prepared. Once the operator's health checks pass, it removes the configured taints and sets a standard node condition that higher-level automation can rely on.

docs.title: The NetworkOperatorReady condition
docs.text:
When all health checks (interface state, reachability targets, API-server access) pass, the configured taints are removed and a custom node condition is created or updated:

Type:    NetworkOperatorReady
Status:  True | False
Reason:  <see below>
Message: Human readable description of the last evaluation.

Inspect it with:

kubectl get node <node> -o jsonpath='{.status.conditions[?(@.type=="NetworkOperatorReady")]}' | jq

Taints are not re-applied on failure

When a health check later fails, the condition is set to False with the corresponding reason, but the init taints are not re-applied — this avoids disruptive rescheduling. The condition provides ongoing status instead.

docs.title: Reasons
docs.text:
Common reasons:

Reason Meaning HealthChecksPassed All checks succeeded InterfaceCheckFailed One or more configured interfaces are not UP (supports glob patterns like eth*, bond?) ReachabilityCheckFailed A configured reachability target is unreachable APIServerCheckFailed Cannot reach the Kubernetes API server

Agent-specific reasons:

Reason Agent Meaning NetplanInitializationFailed / NetplanApplyFailed agent-netplan netplan errors VLANReconcileFailed / LoopbackReconcileFailed agent-hbn-l2 hbn-l2 errors ConfigFetchFailed any Failed to fetch node configuration

docs.title: Configuring taints
docs.text:
The taints removed once the network stack is ready are configured in the healthcheck configuration file (/opt/network-operator/net-healthcheck-config.yaml) under the taints field:

taints:
  - node.cloudprovider.kubernetes.io/uninitialized
  - node.t-caas.telekom.com/uninitialized

docs.title: Splitting configuration across files
docs.text:
The configuration can be split across multiple files using external sources (interfacesFile, reachabilityFile, taintsFile). This is useful for reading parts of the configuration from hostPath mounts or ConfigMaps. Missing files are silently ignored, enabling graceful fallback with FileOrCreate volume strategies.

A sample healthcheck configuration file is provided in the repository at config/samples/net-healthcheck-config.yaml.

docs.text:
